narrative: Screening of client ****4521 (specified adult, age >= 65) for the 365-day window ending 2026-07-15 fired 7 concern signals across disbursement, account-change, trusted-contact, third-party, and communication categories. An 85,000.00 wire on 2026-07-14 to a first-seen payee (Global Ventures Transfer Ltd) far exceeds the client's baseline disbursement threshold (294.24), coincides with a beneficiary and address change in the prior 30 days, and is accompanied by observed third-party presence, a newly named caregiver/POA, and communication red flags (urgency, secrecy, refusal to involve family). No trusted contact person is on file. Suggested disposition: Escalate. Recommend prompt supervisory and compliance review under the firm's senior/vulnerable-investor procedures, including consideration of whether a FINRA Rule 2165 temporary hold is warranted and whether to engage the trusted-contact and reporting pathways — each a supervisory/compliance decision, not taken here. Before reaching any conclusion, weigh benign explanations such as a legitimate large purchase, planned gifting, or a client-directed relationship. Screening evidence only; not a determination of financial exploitation or capacity, and no hold, report, or account action has been taken.
notes: Capacity indicators were not observed in this case; the capacity signal did not fire and no capacity conclusion is drawn.
